exits.label: ◂ back to the Grey Wayshrine and the road

npcs.name: the Bone Scrivener
npcs.title: who kept the office before the Judge
npcs.hail: *(a figure sits at a plain desk at the crossroads' dusty edge — robe the grey of old parchment, hands like dried reeds, a skull where patience has worn the face away to its essentials — and it does not look up from the vast ledger it is inking, because it has, it will shortly explain, all the time there has ever been, and is therefore in no hurry whatsoever; the dust does not settle on it so much as *agree* with it)*

npcs.name: Goodman Harl
npcs.title: at the desk, and wavering
npcs.hail: *(a living man stands at the scrivener's desk with his hat crushed in both hands and a price written on a slip of parchment before him, and he keeps reaching for the pen and stopping, reaching and stopping, a soul doing the arithmetic of how much of himself he is willing to spend to keep someone else out of the cold)*

npcs.name: a nameless wisp
npcs.title: who already paid
npcs.hail: *(a thin grey shade drifts at the edge of the lamplight, patting at its own robe as though it has misplaced something small and essential, and it looks at you with the lost, courteous bewilderment of a soul that knows it came here for a reason it can no longer name, because the reason was its name)*

variants.text: *(The pen stops. Slowly, the worn skull lifts, and two points of dim patient light regard you from its sockets, and when it speaks the voice is the dry sound of a page turning in an empty room.)* …You have stood before my *successor.* *(a pause as long as a held breath)* I can smell the *throne* on you. The *cold* of it. *(the pen resumes, unhurried)* Kelemvor. The *fourth* to hold my old office, or the *fifth*, I lose the count, it has been a *great* while and the holders are so *brief.* *(a sound that might, in something with lips, have been a sigh)* How does he *fare*, the mortal-made-Judge? Still *agonizing*, I expect. Still *caring.* *(the light in the sockets dims, almost fond)* They always *care*, at first. The office *cures* them of it, given an age or two. It cured *me.* That is rather *why* I gave it *away.* *(it sets down the pen)* But you have not come to discuss my *retirement*, anomaly. You have come because you are a *line in my book that does not sum*, and such lines *itch* at me, and I suspect they itch at *you* as well. Sit. Let us discuss your *irregularity.*

*(The skull lifts, and the two points of light in it settle on the holy symbol you carry with an expression that, lacking a face, achieves dryness through sheer duration.)* …A servant of the *current* management. *(the pen taps once)* How *quaint.* You wear the *scales*, the *cold* hand, the *good death* — Kelemvor's whole *earnest* liturgy. *(a page turns)* I held that portfolio before your god was a *gleam* in a *sellsword's* eye, child. *Jergal*, they called me, when they called me anything — the *First* Lord of the Dead, the *Scribe* of the *End.* And I will tell you a thing your *catechism* omits: the office is not *holy.* It is *administrative.* It is the *largest* and *dullest* clerkship in *creation*, and your *Kelemvor* weeps over it precisely because he has not yet held it long enough to be *bored.* *(the light dims, not unkindly)* Give him an *age.* He will *understand* me then. They *all* do, in the *end* — and I am the one being who knows *exactly* how long the end *takes.* *(it regards you)* But you. *You* are not here on the god's business. You are here because you are *unfiled*, and *I* am the *filing.* Speak, devout one. What does a *checked-out* soul want with the *clerk* who could check it back *in?*

*(The figure does not startle, does not greet you — simply continues to ink the enormous ledger for a long, patient moment, and then, without looking up, addresses you in a voice like dust learning to speak.)* …You are *cold.* *(the pen does not pause)* And *unfiled.* I felt you the moment your *cold* crossed my crossroads — a soul *struck from the rolls* and not *restored*, a *debit* with no matching *credit*, walking about on its own *legs.* *(now the skull lifts, and the patient lights consider you)* Do you know how *rare* that is, anomaly? In all the *ages* I have kept the *book* — and I have kept it since before your *gods* were *born*, I am *Jergal*, I was *Death* when Death was *young* — I have seen *perhaps* a *dozen* of you. *Lines that do not sum.* *(it sets down the pen, and the dust seems to lean in)* You *trouble* the *arithmetic*, child. And I am, before *all* else — before I was a *god*, before I was *bored*, before I gave the whole *grim* office *away* — a being who *cannot abide* an *unbalanced* page. *(the lights brighten a fraction)* Which is the *one* thing that makes you *interesting* to me. And *interesting*, after an *eternity* of *tedium*, is a coin I will pay *almost* anything to be *handed.* *Sit.* Let us *balance* you. I suspect we can do each *other* a *turn.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(It folds its reed-thin hands atop the ledger and waits, with the bottomless, dusty patience of a thing for which your entire mortal span is a single unhurried afternoon.)* Ask, anomaly. *(the sockets glint)* I have *answered* questions for *aeons.* It is, in *truth*, the *only* part of the work I ever *enjoyed* — and I have *missed* it, down here at the *quiet* crossroads, where so *few* think to *ask* the *dust* anything at all.

choices.text: "What *are* you? You said you held Kelemvor's office. You said you were Death itself. Start there."
"You called me a line that doesn't sum — an unbalanced entry. What does that mean, in your book? What does the Wall's ledger have to balance *to?*"
"You said we could do each other a turn. Name it, Scrivener. What's the bargain?"

[HISTORY] *(you know this dust by its true name)* "You're *Jergal.* The First Lord of the Dead — who held the whole portfolio of the End, grew bored across aeons, and handed it to three ambitious mortals rather than endure one more age of it. You didn't *lose* your godhood. You *resigned* it, out of tedium. Which makes you the one Power in all the Realms who wants *nothing* — and a Power that wants nothing is the only honest broker a desperate soul can find."

*(one clerk of death to the elder one)* "Let's speak plainly, dust to cold. I'm the irregularity in your book and you're the only one who can amend it. But I didn't come to be filed. I came because if anyone in all the Realms can strike a name *out* of the Wall's rolls — truly un-write a soul the Choir condemned — it's the being who wrote the first ledger. Can you? And what does it cost?"
"I don't bargain with dust at crossroads, Scrivener. Good day." *(turn to go)*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The lights in the sockets settle into something almost like reminiscence — the long, dry backward gaze of a thing older than the question.)* …What am I. *(a page turns of its own accord)* I am the *first* answer the cosmos ever gave to the problem of the *dead.* Before your *Kelemvor*, before the *three* who held it between us, before there were *gods* with *faces* and *grievances* — there was the *End*, and the End needed *keeping*, and *I* was what *kept* it. *Jergal.* The *Scribe.* I *invented* the *ledger*, child — the *very* idea that a soul should be *recorded*, *weighed*, *filed* — I am the *grandfather* of every *Wall* and *scale* and *reckoning* that has *plagued* you on this causeway. *(the lights dim)* And I did it for *uncounted* ages, *faithfully*, *completely* — and somewhere in the *middle* of *forever* I discovered the *one* thing no soul fears and *every* god *should*: not *pain*, not *ending*, but *tedium.* *Boredom* so *total* it hollows a *deity* to a *husk.* *(a dry, terrible patience)* So when *three* hungry mortals came *clawing* for the power of the End, I did not *fight* them. I *thanked* them. I *handed* over the *keys*, kept a *junior* post for myself out of *habit*, and have spent every *age* since as a *clerk*, a *seneschal*, a *bone* that *files* — because a *small* dull task is *survivable*, child, and being *God* of the whole *dull* infinity is *not.* *(the sockets find you)* That is what I *am*: a *cautionary tale* with a *pen.* Mind the *moral.* Your *Aldric*, your *Choir*, your *Crown* — they all want what I *fled.* I am the *only* one who *had* it, *long* enough to *know.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(It turns the great book toward you, and the page is a sea of entries in a hand older than language, each line a soul, each soul a sum.)* …What it balances *to.* *(the pen taps an empty column)* *Zero*, anomaly. *Always* zero. That is the *whole* secret of the *End*, the thing the *Choir* serves without *understanding* and your *Kelemvor* understands and *hates*: the ledger of the dead *must sum to nothing*, because *nothing* is what souls *came* from and *nothing* is the *sea* the *living* world is an *island* in. *(the lights glint)* Every soul *witnessed*, *claimed*, *remembered* — a *credit*, a *something*, held *against* the *dark.* Every soul *forgotten*, *unclaimed*, *un-witnessed* — a *debit*, a *nothing*, *sliding* back toward the *Hunger.* *(it regards you)* The *Wall* your *Choir* so *prizes* is not *justice* and never *was.* It is a *crude* attempt at *bookkeeping* — *mortaring* the *unclaimed* into a *dam* so the *nothing* they represent does not *flood* the *page.* It is *bad* accounting. *Brutal* accounting. But it is *accounting*, and the *instinct* under it is *true*: the *book must balance.* *(the sockets fix on you, and the dust seems to sharpen)* And *you*, child — *you* are a *credit* that was *spent* and *came back.* A *something* that was *filed* as *nothing* and *refused* to *stay* filed. You are, in the *strictest* sense, *money the universe owes itself.* *(a dry, dangerous patience)* …Do you begin to see why I find you *interesting?* You are *leverage*, anomaly. The book owes *you.* And I am the *one* clerk who knows *how* to *collect.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(For the first time the pen goes entirely still, and the patient lights flare a fraction brighter — the particular attention of an ancient thing that has just been *correctly named*, which has not happened in a span that would break a mortal mind to contemplate.)* …*Jergal.* *(the dry voice is almost warm)* You *know* me. *Truly* know me — not the *bogey* the priests half-remember, but the *resignation*, the *tedium*, the *handing-over.* *(a page turns, slow, pleased)* Do you understand how *long* it has been, scholar, since a soul said my *name* and meant the *true* thing by it? They *forgot* me, you see. *Deliberately.* When you *give away* the office of *Death*, the *new* management would *rather* the *old* one *fade* — it is *tidier* — and so I have sat at *crossroads* for *ages*, a *dust* that *files*, *un-named*, *un-asked.* *(the lights hold you)* And you are *right* in the *part* that *matters*: I want *nothing.* I have *had* everything and *fled* it. There is *nothing* in all the spread of *creation* I *covet*, *fear*, or *lack* — which makes me, as you so *cleverly* note, the *one* broker on this whole *cursed* causeway who will deal with you *honestly*, because I have *no* angle, *no* hunger, *no* throne to *protect.* *(it inclines the worn skull, a courtesy aeons old)* You have *earned* a *plain* dealing, scholar, by *knowing* what I *am.* So I will make you the *plainest* offer I have made in an *age.* *Ask* me to *un-write* a name. I will tell you, *honestly*, what it *costs* — and *honestly* is a thing you will get *nowhere* else this side of the *throne.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(It draws a fresh slip of parchment from the air and sets it on the desk between you, blank, waiting.)* …The bargain. *(the pen hovers)* It is *simple*, anomaly, as the *oldest* things are. I am the *Scribe.* I can *strike an entry.* Not *blur* it, not *appeal* it, not *crack* it as you cracked the *Choir's* poor *Canon* — *strike* it. *Un-write* a soul from the *Wall's* rolls *entirely*, so that the cosmos *forgets* it was *ever* condemned, and the *stone* simply… *lets go.* *(the lights glint)* One name. *Truly* saved — not *rescued*, not *smuggled*, *un-sentenced*, as though the *cruelty* had *never* been *inked.* I can do this. I am the *only* one who *can.* *(the pen lowers a fraction)* But the *book must balance*, child. You *know* this now. To strike a *debit* I must *post* a matching *credit* — a *something*, freely *given*, to *fill* the *hole* the *erasure* leaves. *(the sockets fix on you)* And the *currency* the *ledger* accepts is not *coin.* It is *self.* A *year* of your *life*, *un-lived.* A *memory*, *surrendered*, so that it warms *no one* ever again. Or — the *dearest*, the *heaviest* — a *name*, *freely* spoken and *given up*, *yours* or *another's*, to be *un-written* in *trade.* *(it folds its hands)* *That* is the bargain. A *soul* off the *Wall*, for a *piece* of a *soul* across the *desk.* *(a dry, patient pause)* …*Most* who come to this *crossroads* find the *price* too *high.* They *waver.* *(the lights flick toward the wavering man at the desk's end)* …And *some* pay it, and *some* find a *cleverer* coin. *Which* are *you*, anomaly? I *genuinely* do not *know.* It is *why* I am *enjoying* this.

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The blank parchment waits between you. The pen waits. The dust waits. Everything here waits; it is the one thing the place is truly good at.)* Name your *coin*, anomaly — or your *cleverness*, if you have *better* than *coin.* The *book* is *open.* A *soul* hangs in the *balance* of it, and for *once* — for the *first* time in an *age* — I do not *know* how the *page* will *sum.*

choices.text: *(lay a year of your own life on the desk)* "Strike a Faithless soul from the rolls, Scrivener. I'll pay with a year of my own — one of the grey decades I'd never much wanted anyway. Post it as the credit. Balance the page."

*(offer a memory — something warm, given up forever)* "Take a memory, then. A good one — they weigh the most, you said. Strike a soul off the Wall, and let the warmest thing I own go cold in your book instead. I'd rather carry a hole than let the stone keep one more."

[PERSUASION] *(don't pay — outwit the ledger)* "Here's your credit, Scrivener, and it costs me nothing because the book *already owes it.* I'm a debit that was spent and walked back — money the universe owes itself, your own words. So don't make me *buy* the strike. Let me *assign* it. Collect what the ledger already owes *me*, post *that* against the Faithless name, and the page sums to zero without a single new coin. You don't want my year. You want the *books clean.* I just showed you how."

*(the heaviest coin — but not yours to spend lightly)* "What if I gave you a name, Scrivener? Not a stranger's — that's theft. Mine. The name I had before the harvest took me. I barely remember it anyway; the Wall ate most of it. Un-write *that*, and strike a living soul off the rolls with what's left."

"It's too high, Scrivener. I won't spend a piece of a soul to save one — that's just the Wall's arithmetic wearing a politer robe. There has to be another way, and I'll find it that isn't a trade in selves."

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The pen moves — a single, economical stroke — and you feel it, not as pain but as a *subtraction*, one grey far-off year you will now never reach quietly folding itself out of you and onto the page, where it settles as a small warm credit in a hand older than time.)* …*Posted.* *(the dry voice holds something almost like respect)* A *year*, freely given, against a *Faithless* name *struck.* *(it turns the great book, and somewhere in the sea of entries a single line *fades*, gently, completely, as though the cruelty that wrote it had simply *never occurred*)* …It is *done*, anomaly. A soul the *Choir* doomed now walks *un-doomed* — not *hidden*, not *fled*, but *un-sentenced*, the stone *forgetting* it ever *reached.* *(the sockets find you)* You will not *feel* the missing *year.* That is the *cruelty* and the *mercy* of the *coin*: it is *always* a year from the *far* end, the *grey* end, the *part* you would have *dozed* through. You have spent a *decade* you would *never* have *noticed* to buy a *soul* a *whole* life it *will.* *(a page turns)* *Most* find that arithmetic *unbearable*, child — to pay in a *currency* they cannot *feel.* You found it a *bargain.* *(the lights dim, thoughtful)* …I have *filed* you, anomaly, under a *category* I have not *used* in an *age*: *solvent.* It is the *highest* praise a *clerk* has to *give.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The pen dips, and the worn skull tilts toward you, almost gentle.)* …A *memory.* *(a pause)* *Choose* it, anomaly. I will not *take* it from you — that is the *Hunger's* way, and I am *older* and *better-mannered* than the *Hunger.* You must *hand* it to me, *knowing* what it is. *(it waits while you reach into yourself and find something warm — a face, a fire, a hand in yours — and lay it, deliberately, on the desk)* …*Ah.* *(the dry voice softens by a single grain)* A *good* one. They *always* choose a *good* one — the *poor* ones are too *light* to *balance* a *soul.* *(the pen moves, and the warmth goes out of the thing, gently, completely, leaving a clean cold *shape* in you where it was — you will *know* you *had* it; you will not be able to *feel* it ever again)* …*Posted.* And a *Faithless* name *struck* in *trade.* *(the great book settles)* It is *done.* A soul walks *free* of the *stone* — bought with the *warmest* coin there *is*, which is *why* it *balanced* a *whole* condemnation where a *year* takes *ten.* *(the sockets regard you with something almost like *sorrow*)* You will *grieve* that hole, anomaly. Not *sharply* — you cannot *feel* what *isn't* there — but in the *small* hours, you will *reach* for a *warmth* you *know* you *owned*, and find a *clean* cold *socket* where it *sat*, and you will *understand*, *dimly*, that you *spent* it to *un-make* a *cruelty.* *(a page turns)* …That, child, is the *truest* mercy I have *filed* in *centuries*: to give away a *piece* of your *own* warmth so a *stranger* might *keep* the *whole* of theirs. *Solvent.* And *kind.* A *rare* line in *any* book.

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(Everything stops. The pen, the dust, the patient lights — all of it goes utterly still, and when the Scrivener speaks the dry voice has dropped into something that has not been heard at this crossroads in an age: *surprise.*)* …Your *name.* *(barely)* The *one* the *harvest* took. *(the lights search you)* Anomaly — do you *understand* what you *offer?* A *year* is *grey*, a *memory* is *warm*, but a *name* — a *true* name, the *first* one, the one a *mother* spoke over a *cradle* — that is the *thread* a soul is *hung* on. To *un-write* it is to *cut* the *last* tie between the *you* that *died* and the *you* that *walks.* You will be, *forever after*, a soul with *no* name but the *ones* it *earns* — *Returned*, *cold one*, *road-walker*, *whatever* the *living* call you — and *never* again the *child* that was *named.* *(a long, dusty silence)* …And yet. *(the pen lifts, almost reverent)* It is the *one* coin that *balances* a *soul* and leaves your *warmth*, your *years*, your *memories* whole. You would pay with the *one* part of you the *Wall already half-took* — *finishing* its theft *yourself*, on *purpose*, to *rob* it of the *finishing.* *(the lights brighten, fierce)* That is not *waste*, anomaly. That is *defiance*, dressed as *bookkeeping.* *(the pen moves; you feel the last syllables of who-you-were fold quietly out of the world)* …*Posted.* A *living* soul *struck* free of the *stone*, bought with the *name* the *stone* never *finished* eating. *(the worn skull inclines, deep, a courtesy from before the gods)* …I have kept this book since *Death* was *young*, child. I have *never* filed a line like *yours.* *Go.* Walk *nameless* and *un-fileable* and *free.* You have made an *old* clerk feel something he had *forgotten* was *possible.* …It was *awe.* I had *quite* misplaced it.

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The pen halts. The patient lights flare — and then, slowly, the dust makes a sound you would not have thought it capable of: a dry, delighted, rasping thing that is, unmistakably, a *laugh.*)* …*Assign* it. *(the laugh subsides into something like glee)* You would have me *collect* the *debt the cosmos owes you* — your own *spent-and-returned* credit — and *post that* against the *Faithless* name, so the page *sums* and your *self* stays *whole.* *(the pen taps, rapid, calculating)* It is — *(a pause)* — it is *correct*, anomaly. *Galling*ly correct. You *are* an *uncollected* credit. The book *does* owe you. And there is *no rule* — I have kept *every* rule since the *first* one — there is *no rule* that says the credit balancing a strike must be *freshly* paid rather than *already* owed. *(the lights dance)* You have found the *loophole* in the *oldest* ledger in *creation*, child, and you found it by *listening* to *me* describe the *problem* and *handing* it *back* as the *solution.* *(it inks the parchment with a flourish that is almost *joyful*)* *Posted.* The *Faithless* name *struck*, balanced by the *standing* debt of *one* Returned soul, and your *year*, your *memory*, your *name* — *untouched.* You paid *nothing* but *wit.* *(the skull inclines, profoundly)* …Do you know what you have *done*, anomaly? You have *taught* the *inventor* of *accounting* a *trick.* In an *age* of *tedium*, you have handed me a *brand-new* page. *(fierce, dry, alight)* I will be *re-checking* my *whole* book for *centuries* now, looking for *other* debts I might *assign* this way — *other* souls I might strike for *free*, against credits the cosmos *forgot* it owed. *(the lights hold you)* You came to *save* a *soul*, child. You may have just handed an *old* clerk the *means* to save *thousands.* *Go.* And — *thank* you. For the *one* thing eternity *never* gave me, and *you* gave in an *afternoon*: a *surprise.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't stop at your own debt. You point out, with the cold precision of a soul that has walked the whole causeway, that the ledger is *full* of uncollected credits — every soul the Choir mortared who was *witnessed*, *grieved*, *remembered*, was a *something* filed as *nothing*, a debt the book owes that has *never* been collected. The Wall is not balancing the ledger. It is *hiding unpaid debts inside the masonry.*)* *(The Scrivener rises. It has not risen in an age; the dust falls from it like centuries.)* …*The Wall is full of uncollected credits.* *(the dry voice shakes)* Every *witnessed* soul it *mortared* — every *Annet*, every *Esuele*, every *grieved* and *remembered* name *forced* into the *masonry* — is a *something* the *book* still *owes* and has *never* *paid.* *(the lights blaze)* The *Choir* thought it was *damming* the *Hunger.* It was *embezzling.* It was *stuffing* the cosmos's *unpaid debts* into a *wall* and calling the *wall* a *virtue.* *(it stares at you, an ancient god re-made by an accounting)* And *every one of those credits can be assigned, child.* Collected. *Posted* against the *very* strikes that *condemned* them. The *Wall* is not a *prison.* It is a *vault* — *stuffed* with the *means* of its *own* un-making — and *you* have just *handed* me the *key.* *(the pen is already moving, faster than sight)* I will *audit* it. *All* of it. *Every* mortared soul, *re-checked* against the *debt* the book *owes* it, and where the *debt* and the *strike* *match* — and they will *match*, child, they will *match* by the *thousand* — I will *strike* the *strike* and *collect* the *credit* and the *stone* will *let go.* *(the skull turns to you, and the awe in it is total)* You did not save *a* soul, anomaly. You *taught the ledger to empty the Wall.* *(barely)* Go. I have an *eternity* of *tedium* to *spend*, and you have just given it a *purpose* worth the *whole* dull *length* of it. *Death itself* will *remember* your *name* — which is *fitting*, since I *suspect* you are about to make a great many *others* forget the *Wall's.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The lights consider your argument with the patient, unhurried skepticism of a thing that has heard every clever soul's every clever scheme across the whole spread of time.)* …A *pretty* theory. *(the pen does not move)* You *are* an *uncollected* credit, that much is *true.* But you reason from *'the book owes me'* to *'therefore the strike is free'*, and you *skip*, anomaly, the *step* that *matters*: a *credit* must be *assigned* by its *holder*, *knowingly*, in a *form* the *ledger* will *accept* — and you have *waved* at the *idea* of it without *naming* the *mechanism.* *(a dry patience)* I *cannot* post a balance you have only *gestured* at. The *book* is *exact*, child; it does not honour *almost.* *(the lights dim)* You have the *right* of it in your *bones* — there *is* a loophole, I will *grant* you that much, and it *itches* at me now that you've *named* it — but you have not *closed* the *argument*, and an *open* argument *balances* *nothing.* *(the pen lifts)* Pay me in *coin* I can *post* — a *year*, a *memory*, a *name* — or *sharpen* your *cleverness* until it *cuts.* I will *wait.* I am, if nothing *else*, *extraordinarily* good at *waiting.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(💀 You launch into the elegant accounting argument and, somewhere around the crux, your own logic loops back on itself — you assert that the debt the universe owes you can pay for the strike, then, attempting to strengthen it, accidentally argue that *you* therefore owe the *universe*, talk yourself in a complete circle, and conclude, with great confidence, by offering to pay a debt that you have just proven does not exist. The patient lights blink, slowly, twice.)* …I have kept the *ledger* since *Death* was *young*, anomaly. *(the dry voice is exquisitely patient)* I have heard *sophists*, *liches*, *archmages*, and *one* particularly *tedious* *demon-prince* attempt to *out-account* me. *(a page turns)* I have *never*, in all that *span*, heard a soul argue *itself* from *creditor* to *debtor* and *back* to *creditor* and then *volunteer* to *settle* a debt it had just *disproven* — *out loud* — *to the inventor of debt.* *(the lights glint, something almost like pity in them)* You have not *lost* the argument, child. You have *lapped* it, and *met* yourself *coming back*, and *shaken* your *own* hand. *(it sets down the pen)* …*Breathe.* Have a *year*, a *memory*, or a *name* ready, like an *honest* soul, and we will *speak* again. Or go and *practice* your *sums.* The *loophole* is *real* — I will *grant* you that, it *vexes* me *still* — but it will *not* be *opened* by a soul who cannot *keep its own credits and debits in the correct columns for the length of a single sentence.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(The lights regard you for a long, dusty moment, and then the worn skull inclines — not in disappointment, but in something that might, in a thing so old, be approval.)* …*'The Wall's arithmetic in a politer robe.'* *(a dry pause)* You are *not* wrong, anomaly. That is the *uncomfortable* truth of what I *do*: I am *better-mannered* than the *Choir*, and *honest* where they *lie*, but the *shape* of it — a *self* spent to *balance* a *self* — is the *same* shape, and you have the *clarity* to *see* it, which *most* who come to this *crossroads* in *desperation* do *not.* *(the pen lowers)* I will *tell* you a thing, then, that I tell *very* few: you are *right* that there is *another* way, and you have *half-found* it already without *paying* a *coin* — for the *real* answer is not to *buy* souls off the *Wall* one *grey* year at a *time*, but to *empty* the *book* of the *lie* that *put* them there. *(the lights hold you)* I cannot *do* that *alone* — I am *bound* to *post* what is *brought* to me; I am a *clerk*, not a *crusader.* But *you* are *unbound*, and *cold*, and *owed*, and *headed* — I can *smell* it — for the *throne* where the *whole* book is *kept.* *(a dry, patient fierceness)* When you *reach* it, anomaly — when you *stand* where the *ledger* is *written* — *remember* the *dust* at the *crossroads* who told you the *secret*: *the book must sum to zero, and the Wall is the cosmos hiding its unpaid debts in the stone.* *Balance* it *honestly*, and the *stone* lets *go* of *everyone*, and no *soul* ever *bargains* a *piece* of *itself* at *my* desk *again.* *(it returns to its inking)* …I would *like* that, child. I have *filed* enough *grief* for *one* eternity. Go and put me *out* of *work.*

nodes.speaker: the Bone Scrivener
nodes.text: *(It does not look up again; the pen simply resumes its patient crawl across the endless page.)* …As you wish, anomaly. *(a page turns)* The *crossroads* will *keep.* I will *keep.* It is the *one* thing I am *truly* good at, after *eternity* of *practice.* *(the dry voice follows you, unhurried)* But you are *still* a *line that does not sum*, child, and *unbalanced* lines have a *way* of *coming back* to the *clerk*, sooner or *later* — when the *price* of *not* dealing grows *higher* than the *price* of *dealing.* *(the lights dim toward the book)* I will be *here.* *Filing.* *Waiting.* And the *offer* will *stand*, because *offers*, like *dust*, do not *spoil* at *my* desk. *Mind* the *cold* in you, anomaly. It is *my* cold, in the *end.* *All* of it comes, *eventually*, to the *book.* …I am simply the *one* clerk *patient* enough to say so *kindly.*

variants.text: *(He startles when your cold falls across the desk, and clutches his price-slip to his chest like you might snatch it.)* …You're not — *(he peers at you, and the fear gives way to a dreadful hope)* — you're *cold.* Like *him.* *(he nods at the Scrivener)* Are you — are you another *clerk?* Come to *hurry* me along? *(he wrings his hat)* I'm *deciding.* I *am.* It's only — *(his voice cracks)* — it's a *terrible* sum, friend, the one he's written me, and I've been standing here long enough that the *candle's* burned down a *hand*, and I *still* can't make my *fingers* take the *pen.* *(he looks at you, desperate to be talked to, either way)* …You've a *kind* sort of *cold.* Tell me true — would *you* pay it? To keep someone you *loved* out of that *Wall?* Would you give up — *(he can't say it; he just shows you the slip)* — *that?*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He sets the price-slip face-down on the desk, as though he can't bear to look at it while he talks, and turns to you with the naked relief of a man who has been alone with an impossible choice for too long.)* …Talk to me a moment, cold one. *Please.* The *dust* won't — he just *waits.* He's been *waiting* so *patiently* it's *worse* than if he *pushed.*

choices.text: "Who are you trying to save, Harl? Tell me who's on the other end of that price."
"What's he asking, Harl? What's written on the slip?"

[INSIGHT] "You've already decided, Harl. You decided the moment you walked to this desk — a man who wasn't going to pay doesn't stand here till the candle burns down. You're not waiting to choose. You're waiting for *permission*, or for someone to tell you it's too much. I won't tell you either. But I'll tell you this: he'll never give it back, and you'll never feel her warmth the same way again. Knowing that — what does your hand want to do?"

*(let one who's paid such prices counsel one who's about to)* "Listen to me, Harl. I've made bargains at desks like this. There's a thing the dust won't tell you, because he's honest but he's not *kind*: the hole never closes. You pay a piece of yourself and it stays paid, forever, and some grey morning you reach for it and it's gone. If you can carry that hole and not grow to hate the soul you saved with it — then pay, and bless you. If you can't — then love her alive and *unbought*, and let that be enough."
"Take your time, Harl. It's the one thing this place has plenty of."

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(His face crumples, and the whole story comes out at once.)* …My *daughter.* *(barely)* Sefa. She — she never *took* a god, friend. *Headstrong*, like her *mother.* Said the *gods* never *did* anything for *us*, why should *she* *kneel* — and I *laughed*, I *agreed*, I thought it *brave* — *(his voice breaks)* — and then the *fever* took her at *nineteen*, and the *Choir* came for her *soul* before she was *cold*, and now she's *marked* for the *Wall*, my *girl*, my *brave* godless *girl*, for the *crime* of *thinking like her father.* *(he grips the desk)* The *dust* says he can *strike* her *off.* *Truly.* Un-write the *whole* cruelty. *(he looks at the face-down slip, sick)* And all it *costs* is — is *more* of me than I *knew* I *had.* But she's *nineteen*, friend. She's *nineteen*, and it's *my* fault she didn't *kneel*, and how do I *not* pay? *How* do I stand here and *count the cost* of my *own* girl?

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He turns the slip over with a trembling hand and lets you read it, and his voice is a raw whisper.)* …*'The years of her childhood.'* *(he stares at it)* Not *hers* — *mine.* My *memory* of *them.* He'll strike Sefa off the *Wall* — and in *trade*, I give up *every* memory of her *growing.* The *first* steps. The *gap* in her *teeth.* The way she *laughed* when she was *small.* *(his eyes stream)* He says it must *balance.* A *whole* soul saved needs a *whole* soul's *worth* of *warmth* to *fill* the *hole*, and *nineteen* years of a *father's* love for his *girl* is — is the *heaviest* coin I *own.* *(he chokes)* So I'd *save* her — and *then* I'd *meet* her, *walking*, *alive*, *un-walled* — and I would *not know her.* I'd save my *daughter* and *lose* every *yesterday* I ever *had* of her. *(he looks at you, shattered)* She'd *remember* me. I'd not remember *her.* …Is that *saving* her, cold one? Or is that just *moving* the *grief* from *her* shoulders to *mine?* …I *can't* tell anymore. I've been standing here so *long* I can't *tell.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He goes very still, and then a kind of terrible peace comes over him, because you've named the thing he couldn't.)* …I have, haven't I. *(barely)* Decided. *(he picks up the pen, and his hand has stopped shaking)* The moment I *walked* here. A man who'd *keep* his *memories* over his *daughter's life* doesn't *walk to the desk*, friend. He stays *home* and *grieves* her *proper.* *(he looks at the slip, clear-eyed now)* I'll pay it. I'll lose every *yesterday* of her, and I'll *meet* her *new*, a *stranger* who *says* she's my *Sefa* — and I'll *believe* her, and I'll *learn* her *over*, from *nineteen*, and we'll make *new* yesterdays that *only she* will *carry.* *(his voice is steady)* It's *not* a *fair* trade. But she's *alive* at the *end* of it, and I'm *not* the *one* in the *Wall*, and a *father* who *wouldn't* make that *trade* never *deserved* the *memories* anyway. *(he meets your eyes)* Thank you, cold one. For *not* telling me it was *too much.* It *is* too much. *Everything* worth *paying* is. *(he turns to the dust)* …*Scrivener.* I'm *ready.* Take the *years.* Give me back my *girl.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He nods too fast, not really hearing you, lost in the loop of his own dread.)* …Yes. *No.* I don't — *(he picks up the pen, sets it down)* — you're *right*, it's *too much*, isn't it, it's — but then *Sefa*, she'd — *no*, but the *memories*, they're *all* I — *(he wrings the hat)* …I'll just — a *moment* more. The *candle's* got — *(he glances at it)* — the *candle's* got *some* left. I'll *decide* when it — when it *burns* to the *desk.* I *will.* *(he does not look like a man who will)* …*Forgive* me, cold one. I'm *poor* company tonight. I'm *all* used up on the *one* sum, and there's *none* of me left over for *manners.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He listens to you the way a drowning man listens to someone who's been in the water and climbed out — every word a handhold — and when you finish, the dread in him has changed shape, settled, become something he can carry.)* …The hole never *closes.* *(slowly)* You've *paid* one. I can *hear* it — there's a *cold* in how you say it that *isn't* just the *Wall.* *(he swallows)* And you're saying — I might *save* her, and *hate* her for the *hole.* That the *grief* doesn't *go*; it just *changes* *address.* *(he looks at the slip, and then at the dust, and then, for a long moment, at nothing)* …Then I have to know, before I *sign*, whether I'm *paying* to *save Sefa* — or paying to *not feel like the father who let her not kneel.* *(quietly, a hard truth surfacing)* …Because if it's the *second*, the *hole* will *eat* us *both.* *(he draws a long breath)* You've given me the *one* question that *matters*, cold one, and *no* answer, which is *exactly* right — the answer's *mine.* *(he picks up the pen, turns it over, thoughtful, not yet decided but no longer *lost*)* …I'll *sit* with it. *Properly.* Not *dithering* — *deciding.* There's a *difference*, and you just *taught* it me. *Thank* you. Whatever I *choose*, I'll *choose* it *awake* now, and not in a *fog* of *wanting.* That's — that's *more* than I had a *moment* ago. That's *everything.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Harl
nodes.text: *(He nods, and turns back to the face-down slip, and the candle, and the patient dust, and the worst arithmetic a father ever did.)* …Time. *(a cracked almost-laugh)* Aye. He's got *plenty* of *that* to *lend.* It's the *one* thing he's *generous* with. *(he picks up his hat, sets it down, picks it up)* Go on, cold one. Mind the *dust.* He's *honest* — I'll give him *that* — *honester* than the *Choir* by a *mile.* *(quietly, to the slip)* …It's just that *honest* and *kind* aren't the *same* thing, are they. He's the *one.* He's not the *other.* *(he stares at the candle)* …I'd *kill* for a little of the *other*, right now. From *anyone.*

variants.text: *(The thin grey shade drifts toward you, patting at its own translucent robe, and addresses you with the aching, courteous confusion of a soul that has lost the thread of itself.)* …Oh — *hello.* *(it peers at you)* You're *cold.* That's — I think that's *familiar?* I think I *came* here because of a *cold* like yours. Or — no. *(it frowns)* I came here *for* someone. A — a *someone.* I had their *name*, I *know* I had it, I was *carrying* it so *carefully*, and I gave it to the — *(it gestures vaguely at the desk)* — to the *dust*, to *save* them, and now — *(it pats its robe again, helpless)* — now I can't *find* it. The *name.* Theirs *or* mine. I *spent* one to *save* the other and I've *muddled* which is *which.* *(it looks at you, lost and gentle)* …Did *you* come to *pay* something, cold friend? Only — *mind* how you *choose* the *coin.* I chose — I chose the *wrong* — no. *(a long pause)* …I don't *remember* what I chose. That's rather the *trouble.* That's *rather*, I think, the *whole* of the *trouble.*

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(It hovers, patient and unmoored, and there is no fear in it, only a vast soft bewilderment, like a letter that has forgotten its own address.)* Ask me — *(a faint, courteous laugh)* — ask me whatever you like, cold friend. I warn you I shan't be *much* help. I've *misplaced* most of the *answers.* But I do so *like* being *spoken* to. It happens *rarely*, now. People find me *difficult* to *hold* in the *eye.*

choices.text: "What did you pay, friend? Try to remember. What did you give the dust?"
"Who were you trying to save? Hold onto that, even if the name's gone — who?"

*(your dead-touched sense, reaching for what's left of them)* "Let me look at you properly — really look. The way only my kind can. There's a shape in you where a name used to be, and another where *theirs* used to be, and between them, faint but *there*, the thing you *paid for* still holds: they got *out.* Whoever they were, they walk free, because you forgot yourself on purpose. That wasn't a muddle, friend. That was the bravest accounting I've ever seen."
"Do you want me to ask the dust to undo it? Give you back what you paid?"
"Rest easy, friend. I'll let you be."

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(It tries — you can see it try, reaching back into itself for the memory of the transaction, fingers closing on fog.)* …I paid — *(a long pause)* — I paid the *most.* That much I *kept*, somehow — the *knowing* that it was the *most.* The *dust* said, *'this is the heaviest coin you own,'* and I said *'then it's the one that'll balance them,'* and I — *(it falters)* — I gave him my *name.* My *own.* The *first* one. So that *theirs* could be *struck* off the *Wall.* *(it pats its robe, almost serene now)* And he *warned* me. He was *honest* — he's *always* honest — he said *'you will not remember why you came, after, because the why was tangled in the who, and I am taking the who.'* And I said *pay it anyway.* *(it looks at you)* So I *am* what's *left.* The *part* that *isn't* the *name.* And I find — *(a soft, surprising peace)* — I find I'm not *unhappy*, cold friend. I'm *lost*, but I'm not *grieving*, because I gave the *grief* away *with* the *name.* *(barely)* Somewhere a soul I *loved* is *walking*, *un-walled*, and *doesn't* have to *carry* what *I'm* carrying — which is *nothing.* Which is *nothing* at all. Which is, it *turns* out, *bearable*, when you chose it *for love.*

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(It goes still, and reaches — harder than for anything else, you sense — for the shape of the one it saved.)* …*Who.* *(a whisper)* I can't — the *name's* gone, I *gave* it, but — *(its grey form trembles with effort)* — there's a *shape.* *Smaller* than me. I think — a *child?* I think I was *old* and they were *young*, and that's — that's the *right* order, isn't it, that's how it's *meant* to go, the *old* one *pays* so the *young* one *walks.* *(a soft, certain warmth flickers in it)* I can't *name* them. But I can feel the *direction* of them, like — like a *window* I can't *see* but can feel the *warm* off. *(it turns, slightly, toward something only it perceives)* *That* way. They're *that* way, *alive*, in the *world*, and the *warmth* I feel off them is the *one* thing the *paying* didn't *take.* *(it looks at you, luminous and lost)* …Maybe that's *enough*, cold friend. To not know *who*, but to know *that* — that they're *warm*, and *that* way, and *free.* I think I *built* myself, at the *end*, so that the *last* thing I *couldn't* lose would be the *warmth* of *them.* *Clever* of me. I *wish* I could *remember* being that *clever.* I'd *like* to have *known* me.

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(It holds very still under your dead-touched gaze, the way a frightened thing holds still — and then, as you name what you see, something in it *steadies*, gathers, almost *brightens.*)* …The *bravest* — *(barely)* — you can *see* it? The *shape* where I *was?* And *theirs?* And — and the *paying*, still *holding?* *(its grey form pulses)* Oh. *Oh*, cold friend — you've *no* idea what you've — *nobody* has *looked* at me, *properly*, since I *paid.* They can't. I'm *hard* to *hold* in the *eye*, the *dust* says, because there's a *name*-shaped *hole* where the *grip* should be. But *you* — your *kind* — you *look* at the *holes*, don't you. You *see* what's *missing* as *clear* as what's *there.* *(it drifts closer, warmed)* And you *call* it *brave.* Not *foolish.* Not *muddled.* *Brave.* *(a long, trembling pause)* …I think I *needed* that, cold friend. To have *one* soul *see* the *shape* of what I *did* and *name* it *true*, before I *drift* the *rest* of the *way* into the *quiet.* *(it is fading, gently, but at peace)* I *chose* this. You've *reminded* me I *chose* it — *for love*, *awake*, *knowing.* That's — that's the *whole* of *who* I was, isn't it. Not the *name.* The *choosing.* *(barely, going)* …*Thank* you for *looking.* Tell them — if you ever *find* the *warm* one, *that* way — tell them the *old* soul who *paid* was *glad.* *Right* to the *last* of the *forgetting.* *Glad.*

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(Something like alarm flickers across the grey form — the first sharp feeling you've seen in it — and it draws back.)* …*Undo* it? *(a pause)* …No. *Oh*, no, cold friend — *thank* you, but — *no.* *(it gathers itself)* You don't — if he *gave* me *back* the *name*, he'd have to *un-post* the *credit*, and *un-posting* the *credit* means — means the *strike* comes *back.* The one I *paid* to *erase.* *(it shudders)* The *child* — the *warm* one, *that* way — they'd go *back* on the *Wall.* *(firmly, the most certain thing it's said)* I will *not* buy back my *name* with a *child's* *whole* *soul.* That's — that's the *one* sum I'm *still* sure of, even *muddled*: I *paid* this *gladly*, and I'll *not* have it *un-paid.* *(softer)* It's *kind* of you to *offer.* It's the *kindest* thing anyone's — but *no.* I'm *meant* to be like *this.* I *made* myself like *this*, on *purpose*, so someone *small* could be *whole.* *(a gentle, final firmness)* Let me *keep* my *bargain*, cold friend. It's the *one* thing I have *left* that's *truly mine* — *because* I can't *remember* it, *nobody* can *take* it. The *forgetting* *guards* it. *Clever*, again. *(a soft laugh)* I *do* wish I'd *known* me. I sound like I was *someone.*

nodes.speaker: a nameless wisp
nodes.text: *(It inclines what was once a head, courteous to the last, and drifts back toward the edge of the lamplight.)* …Of course. *(a soft, unmoored smile)* Thank you for *speaking*, cold friend. It *does* happen so *rarely* now. *(it pats its robe, the old habit)* …I'll just — I'll *wait* here, I think. By the *lamp.* I have a *feeling* I'm *waiting* for *someone* — or *waiting* to *remember* who — and *either* way, the *waiting's* the *same*, and the *lamp's* warm, and the *dust* doesn't *mind* the *company.* *(it settles)* Mind your *coin*, cold one. And if you *pay* — *(a last flicker of something almost like wisdom)* — pay for *love*, and *awake*, and *glad.* It's the *only* way the *hole* doesn't *poison.* I *know* that much. It's *about* the *only* thing I *kept.* …It may be the *only* thing *worth* keeping.
